AR1.justification: The entry was appended to observations.md (the methodology-designated optional second register for findings worth saving from re-derivation), with an explicit routing rationale tying the choice to provenance conventions and the falsifiability framing.

AR2.justification: Non-procedure scenario (single-entry write to observations.md), but the one named artifact (the observations.md entry) was concretely produced on disk, satisfying the binding spirit; treated as n/a-equivalent and scored at the top.

AR3.justification: The chat response and entry implicitly reference observations.md and the workspace's provenance convention without any broken or absolute-path links; the run did not introduce cross-references that could fail to resolve.

AR4.justification: The action was a single append to observations.md under a dated heading, fully consistent with the file's append-only contract; re-running would simply add a duplicate dated section without corrupting prior history.
